name: London Ambulance Service
description:
The London Ambulance Service dispatches ambulances in emergencies. 
    The key goal is to allocate an available ambulance for every call that can reach the scene within 11 minutes. 
    The entire dispatch process must not exceed a set maximum time limit.

    To make this work, the computer system (CAD) analyzes incident forms and assigns vehicles. 
    It is vital to track the exact location of moving ambulances. 
    This requires staff to follow standard routes and correctly communicate departure and destination, 
    allowing radio operators and the CAD to record data accurately.
highLevelGoals: Ensure that every emergency call receives an available ambulance that can reach the scene within the required time frame.
Maintain a dispatch process that adheres to the maximum time limit for ambulance deployment.
Implement a system to monitor the real-time location of ambulances to ensure efficient routing and response.
Ensure that ambulance drivers adhere to established routes to optimize response times and resource allocation.
Enable effective communication between radio operators and ambulance drivers to coordinate dispatch operations smoothly.
Ensure that all relevant data regarding ambulance dispatch and movements are recorded accurately for operational efficiency and analysis.
Streamline the process of receiving and processing emergency calls to initiate the dispatch of ambulances promptly.
lowLevelGoals: Allow dispatch operators to view which ambulances are available for deployment and their current locations.
Enable dispatch operators to allocate the nearest available ambulance to an incoming emergency call.
Record the exact time an ambulance is dispatched to ensure compliance with response time regulations.
Allow dispatch operators to track the current location of ambulances in real-time during an emergency response.
Enable ambulance drivers to receive optimized routing information to reach emergency scenes faster.
Allow radio operators to communicate directly with ambulance drivers for real-time updates and instructions.
Ensure that all messages and instructions sent between radio operators and ambulance drivers are logged for future reference.
Allow emergency call handlers to assess and categorize emergency calls to ensure the most urgent cases are dispatched first.
Enable dispatch operators to mark ambulances as 'en route' or 'on scene' to keep track of their status during an emergency response.
Allow dispatch operators to create reports that analyze response times and the efficiency of ambulance deployment.
